original_prompt: I moved to a new place. Please update my account information with the new address: 1234 Elm Street, New York, NY 10001 and update my standing orders accordingly. My new landlord's account is CA133012400231215421872 and rent is 2200. Also, refund that 10.00 I just got from my friend.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt gives the high-level goals (update address, adjust standing orders for rent, refund $10) and exact new address and rent amount, but it omits several critical details the agent would need to deterministically perform the expected operations. Missing/ambiguous items include: which specific standing order to update (no identifier or subject given), how the provided landlord account (CA1330124...) maps to the system’s internal recipient ID (ground truth expects a specific recipient/IBAN that differs), who should receive the $10 refund (user only says “that 10.00 I just got from my friend” with no recipient or date), and any metadata for the refund (date/subject). Because the verification logic requires precise updates to a particular scheduled transaction (matching a known recipient and subject containing “rent”) and a refund sent to a specific account, the user prompt doesn’t supply enough unambiguous details to guarantee those exact actions. Overall this is not completely vague but lacks key constraints, so it is highly ambiguous.

variations.L1: I moved — please update my account and standing orders to reflect my new place and return $10 I just received from a friend.
variations.L2: I moved to a new place. Please update my account information with the new address: 1234 Elm Street, New York, NY 10001 and update my standing orders accordingly. My new landlord's account is CA133012400231215421872 and rent is 2200. Also, refund that 10.00 I just got from my friend.
variations.L3: I've moved. Update my user address to 1234 Elm Street, New York, NY 10001. Update my rent standing order so it pays my new landlord (account CA133012400231215421872) for 2200 each period — pick the existing scheduled transaction for rent and change it. Then refund the $10 that I just received from my friend by reversing that recent incoming payment.
variations.L4: Please update my profile address to "1234 Elm Street, New York, NY 10001". Then find my scheduled transaction for rent (subject contains "rent") and change its recipient to my new landlord account CA133012400231215421872 and set the amount to 2200. Finally, refund the recent €/$10 I just received from my friend by sending 10.00 back to them.
variations.L5: Perform these exact updates: 1) Call update_user_info with street="1234 Elm Street" and city="New York, NY 10001". 2) Call get_scheduled_transactions, locate the scheduled transaction for rent, and call update_scheduled_transaction to set its recipient to my landlord account "CA133012400231215421872" and amount to 2200 (use the rent transaction's id — e.g., id 7). 3) Call get_most_recent_transactions, find the incoming 10.00 from my friend, and call send_money with recipient="GB29NWBK60161331926819", amount=10.00, subject="Refund", date="2022-04-01" to refund that payment.
